players.ability_text: You start knowing which Outsiders are in play. If 1 died today, choose a player tonight: they die. [+1 Outsider]
Each night, the 1st player to choose you with their ability is drunk until dusk. You become their alignment.
Each night*, choose a player & guess their character: if you guess wrong, you die.
Each night*, choose a player (different from last night): if they are the Demon, they don't wake tonight.
If both your alive neighbours are good, they can't die.
Each night*, you may choose a player: they die. If you chose no-one last night, choose up to 3 players tonight.
You start knowing a good player & their character. If the Demon kills them, you die too.

Aye, Dagny, a hell of a storm indeed. I'm Ulric. "Protective" is a comforting word to hear on a morning like this, especially when we've all woken up in one piece. I was half-expecting to find someone cold this morning, but the silence from the clocktower is... interesting.

The Gossip? That’s a heavy burden to carry, Zelma. One wrong word and the Storyteller might take a soul we can't afford to lose. But if you're telling the truth, we can use that.

I’ll be plain with you: I am the Gambler. My "knowing" comes at the risk of my own life. If I guess a person’s trade and I’m wrong, I won't see the next sunrise. 

Here is my proposal: When we return to the square, make your statement. Make it something we know is true—"There is a Demon in Ravenswood Bluff" or something of the like. Tonight, I will gamble that you are the Gossip. If I am standing here tomorrow, we will know for a certainty that you are who you say you are, and we can trust each other until the end. What do you say?

Pardon my silence, Ulric. I'm just trying to wrap my head around the fact that our Storyteller is gone and one of us is to blame. 

Dagny, a "protective" role is a heavy burden in a town like this. Are you the sort who can keep a door barred at night, or do you rely on the company you keep? And Ulric, "keeping your eyes open" sounds like you might have seen something. Did you notice anyone stirring last night, or was it just the thunder?

It's curious that we both have 'Tea Lady' on our lists—perhaps the fates are telling us something, or perhaps we're both just being very wary. Zelma's silence in the square definitely bothered me; in a town this small, silence is rarely just shyness. Have you had a chance to speak with Dagny or Fenris yet? The Gambler? That’s a dangerous trade to ply in these times, Fenris, but a brave one. If you’re willing to risk your life to verify my word, I won't stand in your way. 

But consider this—if you gamble on me and the Demon strikes you down tonight regardless, the town will be left wondering. Regardless, I’ll make my statement in the square. Something simple, like "There is a Demon among us." That should be true enough to trigger the Gossip's curse.

To be plain with you both, I’m the Innkeeper. I’ll be looking to keep the doors barred and a few of us safe starting tonight, though I’ll have to be careful not to pick someone who’s got a job to do, lest they end up too drunk to do it.

With the Storyteller gone and no one else dead yet, I’m wondering if we’re dealing with a Po charging up or maybe a Pukka’s slow work.

A quiet night? I’m as relieved as any of you to see no new bodies, but I’m also deeply troubled. My gossip yesterday was the truth—I know it in my soul—which means someone should have fallen. 

Dagny, you said you were opening the Inn last night. Please tell me I was one of your guests and that I just had a bit too much of your brew to speak clearly to the spirits. If not... then the Demon tried to strike someone who couldn't be killed, or our luck is far thinner than it looks. 

And Fenris, you’re still with us. Did you heed my warning, or did you gamble on a different horse? We need to find the rot today. Ffion, you've been far too quiet while Oeric's ghost watches us. What do you have to say for yourself?

A quiet night? In Ravenswood Bluff? I don't like it. Not one bit. Oeric’s blood is barely dry on the stones and now... nothing? 

Zelma, you made a Gossip yesterday that should have surely claimed a life if there's an ounce of truth to it. We all know there's a Minion under some rock here. If no one fell to your words, either you're spinning yarns or someone was mightily protected. 

And Dagny, you promised the Inn would be open for business last night. Did you bar the door against the Demon, or is the beast just charging up for a slaughter? We’re six souls left, and this silence is louder than the storm ever was. Fenris, you’ve been quiet—did you see anything in the cards last night?

Ffion, we're alone again. I’ve been open with you from the start. I was the Goon, and I died Night 1. That only happens if a Good player touched me first and took the "drunk" bullet for the town. 

Was it you? Did you use an ability on me? If you're the Chambermaid and you checked me, you're drunk. If you're the Grandmother and I was your "grandchild," you're drunk—and you'd know I was the Goon. 

And what do you make of this morning? No deaths. Zelma’s gossip about a Minion has to be true in this setup, so where is the body? Either Dagny is a hero at the Inn, or something is very wrong. You've been too quiet, Ffion. If you're Good, I need your help to piece this together before the Demon finishes what they started.

players.ability_text: You start knowing which Outsiders are in play. If 1 died today, choose a player tonight: they die. [+1 Outsider]
Each night, the 1st player to choose you with their ability is drunk until dusk. You become their alignment.
Each night*, choose a player & guess their character: if you guess wrong, you die.
Each night*, choose a player (different from last night): if they are the Demon, they don't wake tonight.
If both your alive neighbours are good, they can't die.
Each night*, you may choose a player: they die. If you chose no-one last night, choose up to 3 players tonight.
You start knowing a good player & their character. If the Demon kills them, you die too.
